I'll analyze the contract carefully:

1. Canonicalization sets width to `max(4, NumDigits(value))` — i.e., minimum 4 digits, but if the value already needs more digits, keep those digits.
2. Kind and value never change.
3. If already canonical (width >= 4 and width == NumDigits(value) when NumDigits(value) >= 4, or width == 4 when NumDigits(value) < 4), it's a no-op.

Wait, let me re-read: "the numeric value is left-zero-padded to a **minimum of 4 digits**. An id already at 4 or more digits is returned unchanged."

So canonical width = `max(PAD, NumDigits(x.value))`. An id is already canonical if its width equals this canonical width.

**(a) Implementation:**

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  var canonicalWidth := if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD;
  Id(x.kind, x.value, canonicalWidth)
}
```

**(b) Lemma with ensures clauses:**

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == (if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= NumDigits(x.value)
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
  ensures x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x) == x
{ }
```

Let me verify the idempotency clause and the "already canonical is no-op" clause carefully.

"An id already at 4 or more digits is returned unchanged" — this means if `x.width >= 4`, then `Canonicalize(x) == x`. But wait, that can't be right if width is, say, 5 but NumDigits(value) is 3 — then canonical should be 4, not 5. 

Re-reading: "An id already at 4 or more digits is returned unchanged" — I think this means if the id's width is already at the canonical width (i.e., `x.width == max(4, NumDigits(x.value))`), it's a no-op. The sentence is describing the no-op condition in terms of "already being canonical."

Actually, I think the simplest reading: canonical width = `max(4, NumDigits(value))`, and the no-op condition is `x.width == max(4, NumDigits(x.value))`.

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == (if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= NumDigits(x.value)
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
  ensures x.width == (if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD) ==> Canonicalize(x) == x
{ }
```
